sutta: and 60 2 the buddha said monks there are these 4 modes of progress what for the painful mode of progress with sluggish higher knowledge abner the painful mode with swift high knowledge the pleasant mode of progress with sluggish higher knowledge the pleasant mode of progress with fifth higher knowledge these are the 4 modes of progress and of what sword monks is the mode of progress that is painful with sluggish higher knowledge in this case a certain 1 is by nature passionately lustful he experiences the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of lust raga likewise he is by nature malicious he experiences the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of ill-will dosa likewise he is by nature deluded he experiences the patch the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of delusion moha in such a 1 these 5 controlling faculties are duly manifested namely the controlling faculty of faith of energy of mindfulness concentration and the controlling faculty of wisdom thus owing to the dullness of these 5 faculties sluggish is is his attainment of the concentration that follows on for the destruction of the asavas this monks is called the progress that is painful with sluggish iron knowledge and of what sort monks is the mode of progress that is painful but with swift higher knowledge in this case a certain 1 is by nature passionately lustful malicious and deluded and he experiences the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of lust ill will and delusion in such a 1 these 5 controlling faculties are manifested in abundance namely the controlling faculty of faith energy mindfulness concentration and wisdom thus owing to the preponderance of these 5 faculties swift is his attainment of the concentration that follows on for the destruction of the asavas this monks is called the progress that is painful but with with higher knowledge and of what sort monks is the mode of progress that is pleasant but with sluggish higher knowledge in this case a certain 1 is not by nature passionately lustful he does not experience the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of lust nor is he by nature passionately malicious he does not experience the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of ill will nor is he by nature deluded he does not experience the perpetual pain and dejection that are born of delusion but in such a 1 these 5 controlling faculties are duly manifested namely the controlling faculty of faith energy mindfulness concentration and wisdom thus owing to the dullness of the 5 faculties sluggish is his attainment of the concentration that follows on for the destruction of the asavas this monks is called the mode of progress that is present but with sluggish higher knowledge and of what sword monks is the mode of progress that is both pleasant and accompanied by swift higher knowledge in this case a certain 1 is not by nature passionately lustful malicious or deluded in such a 1 these 5 controlling faculties are manifested in abundance namely the controlling faculty of faith energy mindfulness concentration and wisdom thus owing to the preponderance of the 5 faculties swift is attainment of the concentration that follows on for the destruction of the ah this monks is called the mode of progress that is both pleasant and accompanied by swift higher knowledge so these are the 4 modes of progress that's the end of the sutta
commentary: this buddha is trying to say and that when we cultivate the holy path we practice to get out of samsara there are 4 types of progress you can either have a painful or a pleasant type and also with either sluggish attainment of the higher knowledges or swift attainment of the higher knowledges and what is the cause of painful progress painful progress is to be found in a person who has these 3 weaknesses like lust anger or ill will or hatred and the third 1 is delusion if these 3 qualities are very strong in a person he is continually uh what do you say he's continually he experiences the pain and dejection due to these 3 things so because of this history things are always bothering him so his progress is always painful but then there are certain sutas where the buddha said when a monk is cultivating the holy path sometimes it can be very painful there can be a lot of frustration there can be a lot of mental suffering in addition to the physical suffering and the buddha said in spite of all that the buddha the monk should grit his teeth and bed bed and and continue to strive and then he'll 1 day he will pass that stage so this is a painful progress in the holy life and due to these 3 factors lust hatred or ill will and delusion and if a person does not have these 3 things to bother him then he makes uh pleasant progress uh if a person does not have strong lust or a lot of ill will or a lot of delusion then the holy life is not so painful it is in fact can be quite pleasant if he knows how to practice and attain the bliss of meditation and then sluggish attainment of the higher knowledges or swift attainment of the highland knowledges depends on the 5 controlling faculties how much how strong these 5 things are in that monk faith energy mindfulness concentration and wisdom so if these are strong there's a lot of stress there's a lot of effort to cultivate these 5 things then the monk will attain the concentration very fast if these 5 things especially the energy the mindfulness and the energy and the mindfulness if he puts a lot of energy and mindfulness in his practice then concentration will come quite quickly and it is said here that the concentration that follows on for the destruction of the asavas so here it is very clear that for the destruction of the asavas you need very good concentration very strong concentration a perfect concentration which is defined in the sutas as 1 pointedness of mind attainment of the janus or sometimes he stated as the 4 janas so it's very clear here that concentration is a very important quality for the destruction of the asavas so that's the suta now we come to another suta to see how this applied
